summary:
Adam and Krista conducted a detailed billing and project tracking review primarily focused on IAS (Integral Ad Science) accounts, with some coverage of Impossible Foods and other clients. They reviewed November and December hours, reconciling discrepancies between AnswerAI one-track work and standard IAS billing. Adam discovered that November had been split into two parts due to remaining budget from October, and that a Part 2 invoice may not have been sent. They worked through the spreadsheet to properly categorize hours between AI/AnswerAI work and standard Help Center support.

The meeting also covered operational improvements for ticket management. Adam emphasized the need for better practices: creating new tickets for post-launch bugs rather than logging time on original tickets, adding 'NC' (no charge) or 'FB' (fixed bid) prefixes to ticket titles for quick identification, and ensuring Jira account fields are set correctly so Tempo time tracking aligns with invoicing. Krista acknowledged IAS scope creep challenges and committed to better ticket discipline.

They also discussed Impossible Foods billing (calculator project at 100 hours fixed bid, transitioning to $250/hour rate), Cameron's availability concerns (described as 'more than MIA'), the need for Cam to attend backlog grooming, and Krista's push for more independence from Adam for both herself and Cameron. The AnswerAI document store ticket was flagged as needing attention due to the upsert vs full refresh issue causing stale data.
keyDiscussionPoints: IAS November billing split into two parts - Part 2 potentially unsent at 128 hours
Reconciliation of AnswerAI one-track hours vs standard IAS billing
October remaining 34 hours rollover into November
December hours lower than average to compensate for high November
New ticket discipline: separate tickets for post-launch bugs, NC/FB prefixes
IAS scope creep challenges with JD and Susan
Impossible Foods calculator project: 100 hours fixed bid at $200/hr (old rate)
IF transitioning to $250/hour on new contract
Document store upsert issue causing stale/hallucinated content
Detailed list of completed work items for IAS transparency
SSO permission ticket dragged on 6 months - Krista force-closed it
Impossible Foods PO number issue resolved (bundled into single PO 002)
decisions: Will present consolidated work item list to IAS instead of separate Tempo exports
New ticket naming convention: FB prefix for fixed bid, NC for no charge items
Post-launch bugs get new tickets, not logged on original tickets
December IAS hours reduced to offset high November billing
Cameron needs to attend backlog grooming sessions
IF calculator invoiced at $200/hr (pre-new-contract rate), 100 hours total

actionItems.action: Send December IAS invoice with reduced hours
Present consolidated work items list to IAS (Nicole/Susan)
Invoice Impossible Foods calculator at 100 hours fixed bid
Get Cameron into backlog grooming and address availability
Ensure Jira account fields properly set for Tempo tracking
Get IF search estimate back to Caitlin
Address document store upsert vs full refresh issue

followUp: IAS response to November/December billing adjustments
Cameron's engagement and backlog grooming participation
IF animation removal project (starting with heroes, bi-component approach)
Document store ownership transfer from Bisma
